title: pyrus pashia var. kumaoni
intro: Pyrus pashia var. kumaoni, also known as the Himalayan wild apple or Kumaon apple, is a variety of the Pyrus pashia tree, native to the Himalayan region and specifically found in the Kumaon region of Uttarakhand, India. It is a deciduous tree with a wide range of medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The plant has been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and digestive issues, and its health benefits include reducing inflammation, boosting the immune system, and supporting digestive health. The herbal preparations of Pyrus pashia var. kumaoni include decoctions, infusions, and powders, which are traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various ailments.
